title: Water Level Sensor Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher's water level sensor (pressure switch) is malfunctioning or sending incorrect signals to the main control board.
fullDescription: Error code 1E indicates the water level sensor (also called a pressure switch or frequency sensor) is not communicating properly with the main PCB. The sensor monitors how much water is inside the tub during each cycle phase. When this sensor fails, the dishwasher cannot accurately determine water levels, which may cause overfilling, underfilling, or a mid-cycle shutdown to prevent water damage.

causes.cause: Defective water level sensor or pressure switch
Loose or corroded wiring harness between the sensor and main control board
Clogged or kinked pressure hose connected to the sensor
Faulty main control board not processing sensor signals correctly

symptoms: Error code 1E displayed on the control panel
Dishwasher stops mid-cycle and drains water
Dishwasher fails to fill with water at the start of a cycle
Dishwasher overfills with water during the wash cycle

fixSteps.title: Power reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher and unplug it from the wall outlet or switch off the circuit breaker. Wait 5 minutes, then restore power and attempt to run a cycle.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixSteps.instruction: Access the bottom of the dishwasher by removing the front kick plate. Locate the small rubber hose running from the tub to the water level sensor. Check for kinks, cracks, or clogs. Blow gently through the hose to clear any debris.

fixSteps.title: Check the sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher unplugged, locate the water level sensor (usually mounted on the right side of the tub near the bottom). Disconnect and reconnect the wire harness to ensure a solid connection. Inspect for corrosion or damaged pins.

fixSteps.title: Replace the water level sensor
fixSteps.instruction: If the sensor tests out of specification or the error persists, replace the water level sensor. Disconnect the wire harness and pressure hose from the old sensor, unscrew the mounting bracket, install the new sensor, and reconnect all connections.

whenToCallPro: If the wiring checks out and replacing the sensor does not resolve the error, the main control board may need diagnosis or replacement, which requires professional service.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher 1E error code fix

faq.q: Can I still use my dishwasher with the 1E error?
faq.a: No. The 1E error prevents the dishwasher from properly controlling water levels, which could cause flooding or poor wash performance. Resolve the error before using the appliance.

faq.q: Is the 1E error covered under Samsung warranty?
faq.a: If your dishwasher is within the standard 1-year parts and labor warranty, the repair should be covered. Some models have extended warranties on specific components. Check your warranty documentation or contact Samsung support.

faq.q: How do I test the water level sensor with a multimeter?
faq.a: Unplug the dishwasher and disconnect the sensor. Set your multimeter to measure resistance (ohms). Place the probes on the sensor terminals. A functioning sensor typically reads between 0 and 25 kohms depending on the model. Consult your service manual for exact specifications.

title: Wash Pump Motor Error
shortDescription: The wash pump motor is not operating correctly, preventing water from circulating through the spray arms.
fullDescription: Error code 3C indicates the wash pump motor has failed to operate or is drawing abnormal current. The wash pump is responsible for pressurizing water and sending it through the spray arms to clean dishes. When this error appears, the dishwasher may fill with water normally but will not wash the dishes because the circulation pump is not spinning. This can be caused by a seized pump impeller, a burned-out motor, or an electrical issue on the control board.

causes.cause: Foreign object (glass shard, food debris, broken dish piece) jamming the pump impeller
Burned-out wash pump motor
Faulty wiring or loose connector between the pump motor and control board
Main control board relay failure preventing power delivery to the pump

symptoms: Error code 3C or 3E displayed on the control panel
Dishwasher fills with water but spray arms do not spin
Unusual humming or buzzing noise during the wash cycle
Dishes come out dirty or still have food residue after a complete cycle

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the dishwasher and disconnect power for 5 minutes. Restore power and run a test cycle. Temporary electrical glitches can sometimes trigger this code.

fixSteps.title: Clear debris from the pump and filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower dish rack and pull out the mesh filter assembly at the bottom of the tub. Clean the filter thoroughly. Check the sump area below the filter for glass, bones, or other debris that could jam the impeller.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the wash pump impeller
fixSteps.instruction: With the filters removed, reach into the sump opening and try to spin the impeller by hand. It should rotate freely. If it is seized or has debris stuck around it, carefully remove the obstruction.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the wash pump motor
fixSteps.instruction: Lay the dishwasher on its back and access the pump from the bottom. Disconnect the pump motor connector. Use a multimeter to test for continuity through the motor windings (typical reading is 5-15 ohms). If there is no continuity, replace the pump assembly.

whenToCallPro: If the pump impeller spins freely, the motor tests good on a multimeter, and the error persists, a professional should diagnose the main control board and associated relay circuits.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher 3C error wash pump fix

faq.q: What is the difference between the 3C and 3E error codes?
faq.a: The 3C and 3E codes are equivalent on Samsung dishwashers. Both indicate a wash pump motor error. Samsung updated the error code nomenclature across model years, so newer models display 3C while older models show 3E.

faq.q: Can a clogged filter cause the 3C error?
faq.a: Indirectly, yes. A severely clogged filter can restrict water flow to the pump, causing it to overheat or draw excessive current, which triggers the 3C error. Always keep the filter clean as part of regular maintenance.

faq.q: How much does it cost to replace a Samsung dishwasher wash pump?
faq.a: The wash pump assembly typically costs between $80 and $160 for the part. Professional labor adds another $120 to $240. Total professional repair usually ranges from $200 to $400 depending on the model.

title: Wash Motor Error
shortDescription: The wash motor is not functioning properly, indicating a pump motor failure or jammed impeller.
fullDescription: Error code 3E is the older nomenclature for the wash pump motor error, now commonly displayed as 3C on newer models. The wash motor circulates water through the spray arms during wash and rinse cycles. When the control board detects the motor is not running, drawing too much current, or not reaching the expected RPM, it triggers the 3E error and halts the cycle. The most common root causes are a jammed impeller, a failed motor, or a wiring fault.

causes.cause: Debris jamming the wash pump impeller
Failed wash pump motor windings
Corroded or disconnected motor wiring harness
Control board unable to supply proper voltage to motor

symptoms: Error code 3E on the display panel
Water fills the tub but does not circulate
Buzzing or grinding noise from the bottom of the dishwasher
Dishes remain dirty after a completed wash cycle

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher or turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Restore power and try running a Normal cycle to see if the error clears.

fixSteps.title: Clean the sump and filter area
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the lower rack and the cylindrical mesh filter. Inspect the sump cavity for broken glass, food particles, or other obstructions. Clean thoroughly and reinstall the filter.

fixSteps.title: Check the pump impeller rotation
fixSteps.instruction: With the filter assembly removed, locate the impeller at the bottom of the sump. Attempt to rotate it by hand. It should spin freely in both directions. If it is stiff or stuck, foreign material may be lodged behind it.

fixSteps.title: Replace the wash pump motor assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water supply. Lay the dishwasher on its back. Remove the base plate screws to access the pump. Disconnect the electrical connector and the hose clamps. Remove the old pump and install the replacement. Reconnect all hoses and the electrical connector.

whenToCallPro: Call a professional if the impeller is free, wiring is intact, and the error persists. The main control board or motor driver circuit may require board-level diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher 3E error code pump motor repair

faq.q: Is the 3E error the same as 3C?
faq.a: Yes. Samsung transitioned from letter-number codes ending in E to codes ending in C on newer models. The 3E and 3C codes both indicate the same wash pump motor error.

faq.q: Why does my dishwasher make a humming noise with the 3E error?
faq.a: The humming indicates the motor is receiving power but cannot spin. This is usually because the impeller is jammed by debris. If the motor hums but does not spin, it can overheat and eventually burn out, so address the issue promptly.

faq.q: Can I run a diagnostic test on my Samsung dishwasher?
faq.a: Yes. On most Samsung models, press and hold the Hi-Temp Wash and Dry buttons simultaneously for 3 seconds to enter diagnostic mode. The dishwasher will cycle through each component and display any active error codes.

title: Water Supply Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher is not receiving adequate water supply, preventing the tub from filling to the proper level.
fullDescription: Error code 4C means the dishwasher has detected insufficient water entering the tub within the allotted fill time. The control board expects the tub to reach a minimum water level within approximately 10 minutes. If the threshold is not met, the 4C error is triggered and the cycle is aborted. This is one of the most common Samsung dishwasher errors and is typically caused by a closed water supply valve, a kinked supply hose, or a failed water inlet valve.

causes.cause: Water supply valve under the sink is not fully open
Kinked or clogged water supply hose
Clogged inlet valve filter screen
Defective water inlet valve solenoid
Low household water pressure (below 20 PSI)

symptoms: Error code 4C or 4E on the display panel
Dishwasher does not fill with water at the beginning of a cycle
Dishwasher partially fills then stops and displays the error
Running water sound is absent at the start of the cycle

fixSteps.title: Check the water supply valve
fixSteps.instruction: Open the cabinet under the kitchen sink and locate the water supply valve that feeds the dishwasher. Ensure it is turned fully counterclockwise (open). If the valve was partially or fully closed, open it and restart the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the water supply hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out slightly to access the rear. Check the braided water supply hose for kinks, sharp bends, or visible damage. Straighten any kinks and ensure the hose has a smooth path from the valve to the dishwasher inlet.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet valve filter screen
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply and disconnect the supply hose from the dishwasher inlet valve at the back. Inside the valve inlet, you will see a small mesh screen. Remove it with needle-nose pliers and clean it under running water to remove sediment buildup. Reinstall the screen and reconnect the hose.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher unplugged and the water supply off, access the inlet valve at the bottom left of the dishwasher behind the kick plate. Disconnect the wire harness and test the solenoid coil with a multimeter set to ohms. A good coil reads between 50 and 500 ohms. If the reading is open (infinite), replace the inlet valve.

whenToCallPro: If the water supply valve is open, the hose is clear, the filter screen is clean, and the inlet valve tests good, there may be a household water pressure issue or a control board fault. A plumber can check water pressure, and an appliance technician can diagnose electrical issues.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher 4C error water supply fix

faq.q: What is the difference between 4C and 4E error codes?
faq.a: The 4C and 4E codes are identical in meaning. Samsung updated the code naming convention over time, with newer models displaying 4C and older models showing 4E. Both indicate a water supply problem.

faq.q: Can low water pressure cause the 4C error?
faq.a: Yes. Samsung dishwashers require a minimum water pressure of 20 PSI (about 138 kPa). If your household water pressure is low, the dishwasher may not fill in time. You can test water pressure with a gauge at an outdoor spigot or contact your water utility.

faq.q: Why does the 4C error appear intermittently?
faq.a: Intermittent 4C errors often indicate a partially clogged inlet valve filter screen or a water supply valve that is not fully open. The dishwasher may fill slowly enough to work sometimes but fail other times depending on household water usage.

title: Water Supply Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher is unable to fill with water within the expected time, signaling a water supply interruption.
fullDescription: Error code 4E is the older designation for the water fill error, now shown as 4C on newer Samsung dishwasher models. When the dishwasher starts a cycle, it opens the water inlet valve and expects the tub to reach a specific water level within a set time. If it does not, the 4E error is displayed. This is typically a supply-side issue rather than an internal malfunction, making it one of the easier errors to resolve.

causes.cause: Water supply valve is shut off or partially closed
Kinked water supply line behind the dishwasher
Clogged inlet valve screen with mineral deposits
Faulty water inlet valve not opening when energized

symptoms: Error code 4E displayed on the control panel
No water enters the dishwasher when a cycle starts
Dishwasher beeps and stops shortly after starting
Cycle indicator lights flash and the dishwasher will not proceed

fixSteps.title: Verify the water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Check that the shut-off valve under the kitchen sink is fully open. Turn it counterclockwise until it stops. If it was closed or partially open, open it fully and try starting the dishwasher again.

fixSteps.title: Straighten the supply hose
fixSteps.instruction: Carefully pull the dishwasher forward a few inches to inspect the water supply hose at the back. Ensure it is not kinked, pinched, or bent sharply. Reposition the hose so it has a gentle curve without restrictions.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply valve. Disconnect the supply hose from the inlet valve at the back of the dishwasher. Use needle-nose pliers to carefully pull out the small mesh filter screen. Rinse it under water and use an old toothbrush to scrub off any deposits. Reinstall the screen and reconnect the hose.

fixSteps.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power and water supply. Remove the kick plate and locate the inlet valve at the bottom left. Disconnect the water hose, the wire harness, and the internal hose clamp. Remove the mounting screws and swap in a new valve. Reconnect all connections and test.

whenToCallPro: Contact a professional if the supply valve is open, the hose and screen are clear, and the inlet valve tests normal with a multimeter. The issue may involve the main control board or a wiring fault.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher 4E error code fix water supply

faq.q: Is 4E the same as 4C on Samsung dishwashers?
faq.a: Yes. They indicate the same water supply error. Samsung transitioned from the 'E' suffix to 'C' suffix on newer models, but the meaning and troubleshooting steps are identical.

faq.q: Can I reset the 4E error without fixing anything?
faq.a: You can clear the error by power cycling the dishwasher, but if the underlying water supply issue is not resolved, the error will return as soon as you start a new cycle.

faq.q: My water supply seems fine but 4E keeps appearing. What else could it be?
faq.a: If water pressure is adequate and the supply line is clear, the water inlet valve itself may be failing electrically. The solenoid coil inside the valve can wear out over time and fail to open even when energized. Test the coil with a multimeter or replace the valve.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher is unable to drain water from the tub, indicating a blockage or drain pump failure.
fullDescription: Error code 5C indicates the dishwasher cannot pump water out of the tub within the expected drain time. The drain pump should evacuate all wash water within about 3 minutes. When the water level sensor still detects water after the drain cycle completes, the 5C error is triggered. This is one of the most common Samsung dishwasher error codes and is usually caused by a clogged drain filter, a blocked drain hose, a garbage disposal blockage, or a faulty drain pump.

causes.cause: Clogged drain filter and sump area with food debris
Blocked or kinked drain hose
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed (new installations)
Failed drain pump motor
Clogged air gap or high-loop issue in drain hose routing

symptoms: Error code 5C or 5E displayed on the control panel
Standing water remains in the bottom of the tub after a cycle
Foul odor coming from inside the dishwasher
Dishwasher stops mid-cycle and will not advance past the drain phase

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door, remove the lower rack, and twist the cylindrical mesh filter counterclockwise to remove it. Rinse it under warm running water and use a soft brush to remove all food particles and grease. Clean the filter housing area inside the tub as well.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher forward to access the drain hose at the back. Follow the hose from the dishwasher to where it connects to the garbage disposal or sink drain. Check for kinks, clogs, or pinched sections. Disconnect the hose and flush it with water to clear any blockages.

fixSteps.title: Verify the garbage disposal connection
fixSteps.instruction: If your dishwasher drains into a garbage disposal, ensure the knockout plug inside the disposal inlet has been removed (this is common on new installations). Also run the disposal to clear any food buildup that may be blocking the dishwasher drain port.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher unplugged and laid on its back, locate the drain pump assembly at the bottom. Disconnect the pump electrical connector and test for continuity with a multimeter (typical reading 15-30 ohms). If the pump shows no continuity or is making grinding noises, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If clearing the filter, drain hose, and disposal connection does not resolve the issue, and the drain pump tests bad or the error persists after pump replacement, call a professional to inspect the control board and wiring.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher 5C error drain fix

faq.q: How often should I clean the drain filter to prevent the 5C error?
faq.a: Samsung recommends cleaning the drain filter at least once a month or more frequently if you run the dishwasher daily. Regular filter cleaning is the most effective way to prevent drain-related errors.

faq.q: Can a clogged garbage disposal cause the 5C error?
faq.a: Yes. Since the dishwasher drain hose typically connects to the garbage disposal, a clogged disposal will prevent the dishwasher from draining. Always run the disposal before starting the dishwasher.

faq.q: What is the knockout plug and why does it matter?
faq.a: The knockout plug is a plastic disc inside the garbage disposal inlet where the dishwasher drain hose connects. On new disposal installations, this plug must be physically knocked out with a hammer and screwdriver so water can flow from the dishwasher into the disposal. Forgetting to remove it is a very common cause of drain errors on new installations.

title: Drain Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher cannot drain water properly, typically due to a clogged filter, blocked drain hose, or faulty drain pump.
fullDescription: Error code 5E is the older notation for the drain error, equivalent to 5C on newer Samsung models. It occurs when the control board determines that water has not been sufficiently evacuated from the tub during a drain cycle. The system monitors the water level sensor after activating the drain pump, and if the water level remains above the threshold after the allotted drain time, the 5E error is set. Common causes range from simple filter clogs to drain pump failure.

causes.cause: Dirty or clogged drain filter
Obstructed or kinked drain hose
Food buildup in the garbage disposal inlet
Worn-out or seized drain pump motor

symptoms: Error code 5E shown on the display
Water pooling at the bottom of the dishwasher tub
Gurgling sounds during the drain phase
Bad smell emanating from the dishwasher interior

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher and remove the bottom rack. Twist the cylindrical filter counterclockwise and pull it out. Rinse under running water, scrubbing with a soft brush to remove grease and food particles. Clean around the filter housing inside the tub.

fixSteps.title: Inspect and clear the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the drain hose from the sink drain or garbage disposal. Place the end in a bucket and run a short drain cycle to see if water flows. If no water comes out, the hose or pump may be clogged. Flush the hose with water to clear obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Check the air gap and high loop
fixSteps.instruction: If your installation uses an air gap, remove the cap and clear any debris inside. If there is no air gap, verify the drain hose has a high loop (the hose should rise to at least the level of the countertop before descending to the drain connection) to prevent backflow.

fixSteps.title: Replace the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and lay it on its back. Remove the base cover. Disconnect the drain pump electrical connector and hoses. Remove the pump mounting screws, swap in the new pump, and reassemble.

whenToCallPro: If basic cleaning and hose inspection do not resolve the issue and the drain pump appears to have failed, professional replacement is recommended unless you are comfortable working with the dishwasher's plumbing and electrical connections.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher 5E error drain not working fix

faq.q: Is the 5E error the same as SE?
faq.a: No. The display font can make 5E look like SE, but they are different codes. 5E is a drain error. If you see what looks like SE, it is almost certainly 5E displayed in a font where the 5 resembles an S.

faq.q: Can I manually drain the dishwasher if it has the 5E error?
faq.a: Yes. You can remove standing water by placing towels in the bottom of the tub to soak it up, or by using a wet/dry vacuum. You can also try lowering the drain hose into a bucket below the dishwasher level to gravity-drain the water.

faq.q: How do I know if the drain pump is bad?
faq.a: Listen during the drain cycle. If you hear a humming sound but no water drains, the pump motor may be seized. If you hear nothing at all during draining, the pump motor may be burned out. A multimeter check for continuity across the pump terminals (15-30 ohms) confirms the motor's electrical condition.

title: WaterWall Reflector Error
shortDescription: The WaterWall reflector motor is not functioning properly on Samsung WaterWall dishwasher models.
fullDescription: Error code 7E is specific to Samsung WaterWall dishwasher models that use a horizontal deflector panel (WaterWall reflector) instead of a traditional lower spray arm. The reflector sweeps back and forth across the tub floor to direct a powerful wall of water across the dishes. When the motor driving this reflector fails, stalls, or encounters an obstruction, the 7E error is displayed. This error does not apply to Samsung dishwashers with conventional spray arms.

causes.cause: Foreign object blocking the WaterWall reflector's path of travel
Failed WaterWall reflector motor
Damaged or disconnected reflector motor wiring harness
Reflector guide rail warped or cracked from heat

symptoms: Error code 7E on the display panel
WaterWall reflector panel does not move during the wash cycle
Clicking or grinding noise from the bottom of the tub
Dishes on one side of the dishwasher come out dirty while the other side is clean

fixSteps.title: Clear obstructions from the reflector
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher and remove both racks. Locate the WaterWall reflector panel at the bottom of the tub. Check that no utensils, broken dish pieces, or food debris are blocking its path. Gently push the reflector back and forth to verify it moves freely.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the reflector guide rail
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the track or guide rail that the reflector slides along. Look for cracks, warping, or debris in the rail. Clean the rail with a damp cloth and ensure the reflector glides smoothly.

fixSteps.title: Check the reflector motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher. Access the reflector motor (typically located under the tub on the right side). Inspect the wire harness connector for loose pins or corrosion. Reconnect if necessary.

fixSteps.title: Replace the WaterWall reflector motor
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher unplugged, remove the base plate. Locate the reflector motor assembly. Disconnect the electrical connector, remove the mounting screws, and detach the motor from the reflector drive mechanism. Install the new motor, reassemble, and test.

whenToCallPro: If the reflector moves freely and the wiring is intact, the motor assembly likely needs replacement. The WaterWall motor assembly is a specialized component and professional installation is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher 7E WaterWall reflector error fix

faq.q: Does the 7E error apply to all Samsung dishwashers?
faq.a: No. The 7E error code is exclusive to Samsung WaterWall dishwasher models that feature the horizontal reflector panel. Standard Samsung dishwashers with rotating spray arms will not display this error.

faq.q: Can I use my WaterWall dishwasher with the 7E error?
faq.a: The dishwasher may still run, but the reflector will not sweep properly, resulting in very poor cleaning performance. It is best to resolve the error before continued use.

faq.q: Are WaterWall parts still available since Samsung discontinued the WaterWall line?
faq.a: Samsung is required to make parts available for a period after discontinuation. Parts can still be found through Samsung parts distributors and third-party appliance parts retailers, though availability may decrease over time.

title: Low Water Level Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher detects insufficient water in the tub during operation, which may indicate a leak or fill valve issue.
fullDescription: Error code 9E signals that the water level inside the dishwasher has dropped below the acceptable threshold during a wash or rinse cycle. This is different from a fill error (4C/4E) which occurs at the start of a cycle. The 9E error triggers mid-cycle, meaning the dishwasher initially filled correctly but water was lost during operation. This can result from a leak in the tub, a malfunctioning drain valve that opens prematurely, or a faulty water level sensor giving incorrect readings.

causes.cause: Leak in the tub gasket, door seal, or spray arm connection
Drain valve opening intermittently during the wash cycle
Faulty water level sensor providing inaccurate readings
Cracked or damaged tub allowing water to seep out

symptoms: Error code 9E displayed mid-cycle
Dishwasher stops during wash or rinse phase
Visible water under or around the dishwasher
Reduced water sounds during the wash cycle compared to normal operation

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Run a short cycle and carefully observe the area around and under the dishwasher for any water leaks. Check the door seal (gasket) for tears, gaps, or debris that could prevent a proper seal. Open the door mid-cycle briefly to check the water level visually.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door gasket
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the rubber door gasket that runs around the perimeter of the dishwasher opening. Look for cracks, hardening, food buildup, or sections that are pulling away from the door frame. Clean the gasket with warm soapy water and a soft cloth.

fixSteps.title: Check spray arm connections and tub fittings
fixSteps.instruction: Remove both racks and the spray arms. Inspect the spray arm mounting points for cracks or loose fittings that could allow water to escape the pressurized system. Also check where the water supply hoses connect inside the tub.

fixSteps.title: Test the water level sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the water level sensor. Disconnect the sensor wire harness and the pressure hose. Test the sensor with a multimeter and compare readings to the service manual specifications. Replace if out of range.

whenToCallPro: If no visible leaks are found and the water level sensor appears functional, a technician should inspect the drain valve assembly and the tub integrity for hairline cracks that may not be visible without specialized tools.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher 9E error low water level fix

faq.q: How is the 9E error different from 4C/4E?
faq.a: The 4C/4E errors occur when the dishwasher fails to fill with water at the beginning of a cycle. The 9E error occurs mid-cycle when water that was already in the tub is lost or the sensor detects an unexpectedly low level.

faq.q: Can a torn door gasket cause the 9E error?
faq.a: Yes. If the door gasket is damaged, water can slowly leak out during the wash cycle, dropping the water level below the sensor threshold and triggering the 9E error.

faq.q: Is the 9E error dangerous?
faq.a: It can lead to water leaking onto your kitchen floor, which may cause water damage. If you see the 9E error, check for leaks immediately and avoid running the dishwasher until the cause is identified and resolved.

title: Button/Switch Error
shortDescription: A button on the control panel is stuck or the control panel is sending continuous input signals to the main board.
fullDescription: Error code bE indicates a problem with the dishwasher control panel buttons. The main control board detects that one or more buttons are being pressed continuously for an extended period, which is abnormal behavior. This can occur when a physical button becomes stuck due to debris, moisture, or mechanical wear. It can also result from a faulty control panel membrane or a malfunctioning main control board that misinterprets signals.

causes.cause: Button physically stuck due to food residue, soap buildup, or moisture
Defective control panel membrane (touchpad)
Short circuit in the control panel wiring
Main control board misinterpreting panel input signals

symptoms: Error code bE on the display panel
One or more buttons on the control panel feel stuck or unresponsive
Dishwasher starts or stops unexpectedly
Control panel beeps continuously without user input

fixSteps.title: Clean the control panel buttons
fixSteps.instruction: Dampen a soft cloth with warm water and a small amount of mild dish soap. Gently clean around all buttons on the control panel, pressing each button several times while cleaning to dislodge any debris or sticky residue. Dry thoroughly with a clean cloth.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle and test each button
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher for 5 minutes, then restore power. Press each button individually to see if any feel sticky, stiff, or do not spring back properly. Note which buttons seem problematic.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the control panel ribbon cable
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher. Open the dishwasher door and remove the screws along the inner edge of the door panel. Carefully separate the outer door panel to access the control panel electronics. Check the ribbon cable connecting the touchpad to the control board for secure connection.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control panel assembly
fixSteps.instruction: If cleaning and reseating the cable do not resolve the issue, replace the control panel (touchpad) assembly. Disconnect the ribbon cable from the old panel, remove the mounting screws, and install the new panel. Reconnect the cable and reassemble the door.

whenToCallPro: If replacing the control panel does not resolve the bE error, the main control board is likely faulty and should be diagnosed and replaced by a professional technician.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher bE error button stuck fix

faq.q: Can moisture cause the bE error?
faq.a: Yes. Steam and moisture can penetrate the control panel membrane over time, causing phantom button presses or short circuits that trigger the bE error. This is more common in kitchens with high humidity or if the dishwasher vent directs steam toward the control panel.

faq.q: Is the bE error the same as bE2?
faq.a: They are related but not identical. The bE error indicates a general button/switch issue, while bE2 specifically indicates a stuck button has been detected for an extended continuous period. The troubleshooting is similar for both.

faq.q: Can I use the dishwasher with the bE error?
faq.a: The dishwasher will likely not operate correctly with the bE error since the control board cannot reliably interpret button inputs. Resolve the issue before attempting to run cycles.

title: Stuck Button Error
shortDescription: A specific button on the control panel has been detected as continuously pressed for an abnormally long time.
fullDescription: Error code bE2 is a more specific variant of the bE error. While bE indicates a general button malfunction, bE2 specifically means the main control board has detected a single button sending a continuous signal for longer than the allowed threshold (typically more than 30 seconds). This is usually caused by a physically stuck button, a defective membrane switch under that button, or moisture intrusion in the control panel assembly.

causes.cause: Single button physically jammed by debris or residue
Membrane switch failure under a specific button
Moisture or condensation inside the control panel
Ribbon cable partially damaged at specific pin positions

symptoms: Error code bE2 on the display
A specific button appears stuck or depressed
Dishwasher activates functions on its own
Unable to select specific wash cycles or options

fixSteps.title: Identify and clean the stuck button
fixSteps.instruction: Press each button on the control panel and feel for any that do not click properly or feel sticky. Use a cloth dampened with warm soapy water to clean around the problem button. Press it repeatedly while cleaning to work out any debris.

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power for at least 10 minutes to allow the control board to fully reset. This also gives any moisture inside the panel time to dissipate. Restore power and check if the error returns.

fixSteps.title: Dry out the control panel
fixSteps.instruction: If moisture is suspected, use a hair dryer on a low heat setting to gently warm the control panel area for 5-10 minutes. This helps evaporate any trapped moisture inside the panel membrane.

fixSteps.title: Replace the control panel membrane
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher. Open the door and remove the inner door panel screws to access the control panel. Disconnect the ribbon cable, remove the old membrane panel, and install the replacement. Reassemble the door panel.

whenToCallPro: If the control panel has been replaced and the bE2 error still appears, the problem lies in the main control board, which requires professional diagnosis and repair.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher bE2 stuck button error fix

faq.q: Why does my dishwasher get the bE2 error after running a steam-heavy cycle?
faq.a: Steamy cycles can cause condensation to form inside the control panel. The moisture creates phantom connections in the membrane switch, making the board think a button is pressed. Allowing the panel to dry out or using a hair dryer on low heat often resolves this.

faq.q: Can I replace just the membrane switch without the whole panel?
faq.a: On most Samsung dishwashers, the membrane switch and the touchpad panel are sold as one integrated assembly. Individual switch replacement is not practical for consumer repair.

faq.q: How do I prevent the bE2 error from recurring?
faq.a: Keep the control panel clean and dry. Wipe down the panel after each wash cycle if condensation is present. Ensure the dishwasher door vents steam away from the control panel. Avoid spraying water directly on the panel during kitchen cleaning.

title: Communication Error
shortDescription: There is a communication failure between the main control board and the sub-control board or user interface panel.
fullDescription: Error code CE indicates a communication breakdown between the dishwasher's main control board (PCB) and the display/user interface board. These two boards exchange data through a serial communication bus via a wiring harness or ribbon cable. When the main board fails to receive expected responses from the display board (or vice versa), the CE error is triggered. This can be caused by a loose connector, damaged wiring, electromagnetic interference, or a failed circuit board.

causes.cause: Loose or corroded connector between the main board and display board
Damaged communication wiring harness
Failed display/interface control board
Failed main control board communication circuit

symptoms: Error code CE on the display
Display panel shows garbled or partial information
Dishwasher does not respond to button presses
Cycle starts but control panel goes blank

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the circuit breaker supplying the dishwasher for 10 minutes. This performs a full reset of both control boards. Restore power and check if the CE error clears.

fixSteps.title: Reseat the communication connectors
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher. Open the door and remove the screws along the inner door panel to separate the outer panel. Locate the ribbon cable or wire harness connecting the display board to the main board. Disconnect and firmly reconnect each connector.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the wiring harness
fixSteps.instruction: With the door panel open, visually inspect the communication cable for damage such as pinched wires, fraying, burn marks, or corroded pins. Replace the harness if any damage is found.

fixSteps.title: Replace the display board or main control board
fixSteps.instruction: If reseating and inspecting the wiring does not resolve the error, one of the boards has failed. Start by replacing the display board (less expensive). If the error persists, replace the main control board.

whenToCallPro: If reseating the connectors does not resolve the CE error, professional board-level diagnosis is recommended. Replacing the wrong board is costly, so having a technician properly diagnose which board has failed can save money.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher CE communication error fix

faq.q: Can a power surge cause the CE error?
faq.a: Yes. Electrical surges or power fluctuations can damage communication circuits on the control boards. If the CE error appeared after a power outage or storm, a surge may have damaged one of the boards.

faq.q: Is it worth repairing a CE error or should I replace the dishwasher?
faq.a: If the dishwasher is less than 5 years old, repair is usually worthwhile. A display board replacement is relatively affordable. If the main control board needs replacement, compare the total repair cost (parts plus labor) to the price of a new dishwasher.

faq.q: Can electromagnetic interference cause the CE error?
faq.a: In rare cases, strong electromagnetic interference from nearby appliances or improperly grounded electrical circuits can disrupt communication between the boards. Ensure the dishwasher is on a dedicated circuit and properly grounded.

title: Heater Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher's water heating element has malfunctioned, preventing water from reaching the required wash temperature.
fullDescription: Error code HE indicates the dishwasher's heating system is not working properly. The heater is responsible for raising the water temperature to the optimal level for effective cleaning and sanitizing (typically 120-150 degrees F depending on the cycle). The control board monitors the water temperature via a thermistor and expects the temperature to rise within a specific timeframe after the heater is activated. If the temperature does not increase as expected, the HE error is triggered.

causes.cause: Burned-out heating element
Faulty thermistor (temperature sensor) providing incorrect readings
Loose or corroded wiring connections to the heater
Main control board relay not supplying power to the heater

symptoms: Error code HE or HE1 on the display panel
Water inside the dishwasher remains cold during wash cycles
Dishes come out with greasy residue or food still attached
Sanitize cycle does not reach the required temperature

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher or shut off the breaker for 5 minutes. Restore power and run a Normal cycle. If the HE error was caused by a temporary sensor glitch, the reset may clear it.

fixSteps.title: Check the hot water supply
fixSteps.instruction: Before suspecting the heater, ensure your home hot water heater is set to at least 120 degrees F. Run the kitchen faucet hot water for 2 minutes before starting the dishwasher to ensure hot water is readily available at the supply line.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the heating element from below by removing the base plate. Disconnect the wires from the heater terminals. Use a multimeter set to ohms to measure resistance across the terminals. A functioning element typically reads between 10 and 30 ohms. An open circuit (infinite resistance) means the element is burned out.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher unplugged and water off, remove the base plate. Disconnect the wiring from the old heater terminals. Remove the retaining nut or clips securing the heater to the tub. Carefully pull the old heater out through the bottom. Install the new heater, secure the retaining hardware, and reconnect the wires.

whenToCallPro: If both the heating element and thermistor test good but the HE error persists, the main control board's heater relay may be faulty. This requires board-level testing by a professional.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher HE heater error fix

faq.q: Can cold water supply cause the HE error?
faq.a: While cold inlet water alone does not typically trigger the HE error, it forces the heater to work much harder. If the heater is already marginal, starting with cold water may prevent it from reaching the target temperature in time, triggering the error. Always run hot water at the faucet before starting the dishwasher.

faq.q: Is the HE error dangerous?
faq.a: A failed heating element itself is not dangerous. However, if the wiring to the heater has shorted or melted, there could be a fire risk. If you notice any burning smell or discolored wires, disconnect power immediately and call a professional.

faq.q: What is the difference between HE and HE1?
faq.a: Both codes indicate a heater malfunction. HE is the general heater error, while HE1 may appear on certain models to indicate the specific heating element (as opposed to the thermistor). The troubleshooting steps are the same.

title: Heater Element Error
shortDescription: The heating element specifically has failed or is not receiving power, preventing water temperature from rising.
fullDescription: Error code HE1 specifically points to the heating element circuit rather than the temperature sensor. While the HE error can be triggered by either a heater or thermistor fault, HE1 is generated when the control board detects no current draw from the heating element after activating the heater relay. This strongly suggests the heating element itself is open (burned out) or the wiring to the element is broken. The dishwasher cannot properly sanitize or dissolve detergent without adequate water temperature.

causes.cause: Heating element burned out or open circuit
Broken or disconnected wire to the heating element
Corroded heating element terminals
Heater relay on the main control board has failed

symptoms: Error code HE1 on the display
Water temperature remains cold throughout the entire cycle
Dishes are not clean, with greasy residue remaining
Drying performance is significantly reduced

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the dishwasher from power for 5 minutes. Restore power and start a test cycle. If the error returns quickly, proceed to the next step.

fixSteps.title: Access and inspect the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher. Remove the kick plate at the bottom. Locate the heating element terminals (two metal posts protruding from the bottom of the tub with wires attached). Inspect for scorched wires, melted connectors, or corrosion on the terminals.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element for continuity
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the wires from both heating element terminals. Set your multimeter to the ohms setting and place one probe on each terminal. A good heating element reads 10-30 ohms. If the reading is infinite (OL), the element is burned out and needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Replace the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the retaining nut or bracket from the element terminals underneath the tub. Push the old element into the tub and remove it. Insert the new element through the openings, secure with the retaining hardware from below, and reconnect the wires.

whenToCallPro: If the heating element and wiring are both intact but the HE1 error persists, the heater relay on the main control board is likely failed. This requires a professional to diagnose and replace the control board.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher HE1 heating element replacement

faq.q: How long do Samsung dishwasher heating elements last?
faq.a: Heating elements typically last 5-10 years depending on usage patterns, water quality, and how often high-temperature cycles are used. Hard water with high mineral content can shorten heater life due to scale buildup.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher without the heater working?
faq.a: Technically the dishwasher may still run some cycles, but cleaning performance will be very poor. Hot water is essential for dissolving detergent, removing grease, and sanitizing dishes. Running without heat also increases the risk of bacterial growth.

faq.q: Is replacing the heating element a DIY-friendly repair?
faq.a: Yes, it is one of the more accessible Samsung dishwasher repairs. The element is accessed from under the dishwasher by removing the kick plate. The main challenges are disconnecting the old element and ensuring a watertight seal with the new one.

title: Leak Error / Water Leak Detected
shortDescription: The leak sensor in the base pan has detected water, indicating a leak somewhere in the dishwasher system.
fullDescription: Error code LE means the dishwasher's leak sensor (located in the base pan beneath the tub) has detected water where it should not be. Samsung dishwashers have a float switch or moisture sensor in the base tray that triggers this error as a safety measure to prevent water damage to your kitchen floor and cabinetry. When activated, the dishwasher immediately stops the wash cycle and activates the drain pump to remove water. Common causes include a leaking door gasket, loose hose clamp, cracked sump, or a faulty sensor.

causes.cause: Worn or damaged door gasket allowing water to seep into the base pan
Loose or cracked water hose connection inside the dishwasher
Cracked sump or tub allowing water into the base area
Leak sensor malfunction (false alarm due to moisture or sensor failure)

symptoms: Error code LE or LC on the display
Dishwasher stops mid-cycle and begins draining
Water visible under the dishwasher on the kitchen floor
Drain pump runs continuously even when the dishwasher is idle

fixSteps.title: Check for visible leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out from the counter if possible. Look under and around the dishwasher for water. Run a short cycle and observe where water appears. Place paper towels around the base to help pinpoint the leak location.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the door gasket
fixSteps.instruction: Examine the rubber gasket around the door opening. Look for tears, gaps, hardened sections, or food debris preventing a proper seal. Clean the gasket and the mating surface on the tub with a damp cloth. Replace the gasket if damaged.

fixSteps.title: Check internal hose connections
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and remove the kick plate. Inspect all visible hose connections under the tub for drips or loose clamps. Tighten any loose hose clamps with pliers. Check the drain hose, supply hose, and recirculation hose connections.

fixSteps.title: Inspect and dry the base pan and leak sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Tilt the dishwasher back carefully and use towels or a wet/dry vacuum to remove any water from the base pan. Locate the leak sensor float switch in the base pan and ensure it moves freely. If the sensor was triggered by residual water and no active leak exists, drying the pan may clear the error.

whenToCallPro: If you cannot identify the source of the leak after inspecting the door gasket and hose connections, or if the leak appears to be coming from the tub itself (cracked sump), call a professional. Tub cracks may require specialized sealing or tub replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher LE leak error fix

faq.q: Can overfilling detergent cause the LE error?
faq.a: Yes. Using too much detergent or the wrong type of detergent can cause excessive suds that push past the door gasket into the base pan, triggering the leak sensor. Always use dishwasher-specific detergent in the recommended amount.

faq.q: Why does my dishwasher drain pump keep running with the LE error?
faq.a: When the leak sensor is triggered, the dishwasher enters a safety mode where the drain pump runs continuously to remove water and prevent flooding. The pump will continue running until the sensor float switch drops back to its normal position or the dishwasher is unplugged.

faq.q: Is the LE error different from the LC error?
faq.a: The LE and LC codes both indicate a leak detection. LC is the newer nomenclature used on more recent Samsung models, while LE is found on older models. The meaning and troubleshooting steps are identical.

title: Leak Detected Error
shortDescription: The leak detection sensor has been activated, indicating water has accumulated in the dishwasher base pan.
fullDescription: Error code LC is the updated version of the LE error code used on newer Samsung dishwasher models. It functions identically, alerting the user that the leak sensor float switch in the base pan has risen above its threshold, indicating water accumulation. The dishwasher immediately halts its current operation and may engage the drain pump to evacuate water. This is a critical safety feature that prevents water damage to kitchen floors and cabinetry.

causes.cause: Deteriorated door gasket allowing water past the seal
Loose internal hose clamp causing a slow drip
Excess suds from wrong detergent type or excessive detergent
Faulty leak sensor float switch giving false readings

symptoms: Error code LC displayed on the panel
Dishwasher shuts down mid-cycle unexpectedly
Water puddle found under the dishwasher
Drain pump runs continuously without user initiation

fixSteps.title: Mop up and inspect for active leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and unplug the dishwasher. Use towels to mop up any standing water under and around the unit. Pull the dishwasher out if accessible to inspect the rear connections.

fixSteps.title: Clean and inspect the door gasket
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the full perimeter of the door gasket for damage, debris, or wear. Clean the gasket thoroughly with warm water and a cloth. Check that the door closes tightly and the latch engages fully.

fixSteps.title: Dry the base pan and check the float switch
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher pulled out and unplugged, tilt it back slightly to access the base pan. Use towels or a shop vac to remove all water. Locate the float switch and ensure it moves up and down freely. A stuck float can cause false LC errors.

fixSteps.title: Tighten or replace internal hose clamps
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all hose connections visible from below the tub. Use pliers to tighten spring clamps or a screwdriver for screw-type clamps. If a hose is cracked or a clamp is broken, replace it. Run a test cycle to verify the leak is resolved.

whenToCallPro: If no external leak source can be identified, water may be coming from an internal tub crack or the sump assembly, which requires professional disassembly and inspection.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher LC leak error code fix

faq.q: Can I reset the LC error after drying the base pan?
faq.a: Yes. Once you dry the base pan and the float switch returns to its resting position, power cycling the dishwasher should clear the LC error. However, if the underlying leak is not fixed, the error will return.

faq.q: Is it safe to tilt my dishwasher to dry the base pan?
faq.a: Yes, you can carefully tilt the dishwasher backward to access the base pan, but first disconnect the power and water supply. Have a helper support the unit and be careful not to strain the drain hose or power cord.

faq.q: Why does the LC error appear even when I see no water on the floor?
faq.a: A very small internal leak may drip into the base pan without reaching the floor. The base pan is designed to contain small amounts of water and trigger the sensor before it overflows. Also, the float switch can occasionally stick in the raised position if it gets dirty.

title: Over-Level Water Error / Overflow
shortDescription: The dishwasher has detected an excessively high water level in the tub, indicating an overfill condition.
fullDescription: Error code OC means the dishwasher has detected that the water level in the tub has risen above the safe operating maximum. This is a critical safety error. The overflow sensor triggers when water reaches a level that could potentially leak out through the door. Common causes include a stuck-open water inlet valve that continues filling even after the control board signals it to close, a drain blockage that prevents normal water evacuation, or a water level sensor malfunction.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve stuck in the open position, continuously filling
Blocked drain preventing normal water removal during cycle transitions
Faulty water level sensor not signaling the fill to stop
Main control board continuing to energize the inlet valve incorrectly

symptoms: Error code OC or OE on the display
Water visibly above the door seal line inside the tub
Water leaking from the dishwasher door
Dishwasher continuously fills with water even when cycle is stopped

fixSteps.title: Emergency water shutoff
fixSteps.instruction: Immediately turn off the water supply valve under the kitchen sink. This stops any additional water from entering the dishwasher. Then unplug the dishwasher or switch off the circuit breaker.

fixSteps.title: Manually drain excess water
fixSteps.instruction: Use a wet/dry vacuum or towels to remove excess water from the tub. Alternatively, lower the drain hose into a bucket below the dishwasher level to gravity-drain. Remove as much water as possible.

fixSteps.title: Check the drain system
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the drain filter, inspect the drain hose for clogs, and verify the garbage disposal connection is clear. A blocked drain can cause water to accumulate during cycle transitions, triggering the OC error.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: With the water supply off and dishwasher unplugged, access the inlet valve behind the kick plate. Turn the water supply on briefly while the dishwasher is off. If water continues to flow into the tub, the inlet valve is stuck open and must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve, drain system, and water level sensor all appear functional, the main control board may be incorrectly managing the fill cycle. Professional diagnosis of the control board is recommended.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher OC overflow error fix

faq.q: Is the OC error an emergency?
faq.a: It can be. If water is overflowing from the dishwasher, shut off the water supply valve immediately. The OC error means water levels are dangerously high and could cause water damage to your kitchen.

faq.q: Can a stuck inlet valve cause flooding?
faq.a: Yes. If the water inlet valve is stuck open, water will continuously flow into the dishwasher regardless of whether it is running a cycle. This can cause the tub to overflow and flood your kitchen. If you suspect a stuck valve, turn off the water supply immediately.

faq.q: How do I test if my inlet valve is stuck open?
faq.a: Turn off the water supply, unplug the dishwasher, and open the door to observe the tub. Turn the water supply back on. If water flows into the tub even though the dishwasher is unplugged and off, the inlet valve is mechanically stuck open and must be replaced.

title: Overflow Error
shortDescription: The dishwasher tub has exceeded the maximum safe water level, potentially due to a stuck inlet valve or drain blockage.
fullDescription: Error code OE is the older notation for the overflow condition, now displayed as OC on newer Samsung models. This error indicates the water level inside the tub has exceeded the overflow threshold. The dishwasher will immediately stop all operations and attempt to drain to prevent water from spilling over the door seal. This is a safety-critical error that requires immediate attention to prevent water damage.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve mechanically stuck open
Drain hose or pump blockage preventing water evacuation
Water level pressure sensor malfunction
Control board fill cycle logic error

symptoms: Error code OE on the display
Excessive water visible in the tub
Water dripping from the door seal
Dishwasher attempts to drain but the error persists

fixSteps.title: Shut off the water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Close the water supply valve under the sink that feeds the dishwasher. Unplug the dishwasher. This stops any further water from entering.

fixSteps.title: Remove excess water
fixSteps.instruction: Use a wet/dry vacuum, large sponge, or towels to bail out excess water from the tub. Open the door carefully as water may be near the top of the tub.

fixSteps.title: Clear the drain system
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the drain filter. Disconnect and flush the drain hose. Check the garbage disposal inlet for blockages. Ensure the drain path is completely clear.

fixSteps.title: Replace the inlet valve if stuck
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher unplugged, turn the water supply back on briefly. If water enters the tub, the inlet valve is stuck open. Turn the water off, remove the old valve from behind the kick plate, and install a replacement.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve and drain system are both functioning properly, the overflow may be caused by a control board malfunction that improperly manages the fill cycle. Professional control board diagnosis is needed.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher OE overflow error code fix

faq.q: Is the OE error the same as OC?
faq.a: Yes. OE is the older code and OC is the newer version. Both indicate an overflow or excessively high water level condition. Troubleshooting is identical.

faq.q: Will the OC/OE error cause permanent damage?
faq.a: If caught quickly, no permanent damage should occur. The dishwasher's leak sensor and overflow detection are designed to prevent catastrophic flooding. However, repeated overfilling can damage the door seal, control board, and surrounding cabinetry over time.

faq.q: Can a power outage cause the OE error?
faq.a: A power outage during the fill phase could leave the dishwasher in an uncertain state. When power returns, it may detect water at an unexpected level and trigger the OE error. In this case, power cycling the dishwasher and running a drain-only cycle usually resolves it.

title: Half-Load Function Error
shortDescription: The zone wash or half-load function is malfunctioning, typically due to a faulty diverter motor or valve.
fullDescription: Error code PE relates to the zone wash (half-load) function available on select Samsung dishwasher models. This feature uses a diverter valve to direct water flow to either the upper or lower rack area for energy-efficient partial-load washing. The PE error is triggered when the diverter motor or valve fails to actuate properly, or the position sensor does not confirm the valve has moved to the expected position. The dishwasher may still operate on full-wash cycles, but the half-load function will not work correctly.

causes.cause: Diverter motor seized or burned out
Diverter valve stuck due to mineral buildup or debris
Position sensor on the diverter assembly is faulty
Wiring issue between the diverter motor and main control board

symptoms: Error code PE on the display panel
Half-load or zone wash option does not function
Clicking noise from the diverter motor area
Only upper or lower rack receives water when zone wash is not selected

fixSteps.title: Reset the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher for 5 minutes and restore power. Run a full Normal cycle (not a half-load cycle) to see if the error clears. If the PE error only appears when using the half-load function, the diverter may be the issue.

fixSteps.title: Run a cycle with vinegar to clear mineral deposits
fixSteps.instruction: Place a cup of white vinegar on the top rack and run a hot water cycle. Vinegar can help dissolve mineral deposits that may be causing the diverter valve to stick.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the diverter motor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the diverter motor (typically located on the left side of the sump assembly under the tub). Check the wire connector for loose connections or corrosion. Reconnect firmly.

fixSteps.title: Replace the diverter motor assembly
fixSteps.instruction: With the dishwasher unplugged, access the diverter motor. Disconnect the electrical connector, remove the mounting screws, and detach the motor from the diverter valve. Install the replacement motor and reassemble.

whenToCallPro: If you are unable to locate the diverter motor or if the motor replacement does not clear the PE error, a technician should inspect the diverter valve mechanism and control board signals.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher PE error diverter motor fix

faq.q: Can I still use my dishwasher with the PE error?
faq.a: In many cases, yes. The PE error primarily affects the half-load or zone wash function. Full wash cycles may still operate normally. However, if the diverter is stuck in a position that only sends water to one section, full cycles may also be affected.

faq.q: Do all Samsung dishwashers have the zone wash feature?
faq.a: No. The zone wash or half-load feature is only available on select mid-range and premium Samsung dishwasher models. Entry-level models do not have this feature and will not display the PE error.

faq.q: What does the diverter motor do?
faq.a: The diverter motor controls a valve that directs water flow within the dishwasher. In zone wash mode, it routes water to only the upper or lower spray area. During full cycles, it ensures water reaches all spray arms evenly.

title: Temperature Sensor Error
shortDescription: The thermistor (temperature sensor) is providing abnormal readings, preventing accurate water temperature monitoring.
fullDescription: Error code tE indicates that the thermistor (temperature sensor) is not providing expected resistance values to the main control board. The thermistor is a small sensor that measures water temperature inside the dishwasher and communicates with the control board to regulate heating. When the sensor is open, shorted, or reading far outside the expected range, the tE error is triggered. Without accurate temperature data, the dishwasher cannot properly control the heating element or determine when the water has reached the target temperature.

causes.cause: Faulty thermistor (open circuit or shorted)
Loose or disconnected thermistor wiring
Corroded thermistor connector pins
Main control board temperature monitoring circuit failure

symptoms: Error code tE on the display panel
Dishwasher does not heat water or overheats water
Cycle times are abnormally long (dishwasher waits for temperature that is never reached or detected)
Sanitize and heavy wash cycles fail to complete

fixSteps.title: Power cycle the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher for 5 minutes to reset the control board. Restore power and run a Normal cycle. Temporary sensor glitches can sometimes be cleared with a reset.

fixSteps.title: Check the thermistor connection
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and locate the thermistor. On most Samsung models, it is attached to the sump area underneath the tub. Inspect the wire connector for looseness or corrosion. Disconnect and reconnect the plug firmly.

fixSteps.title: Test the thermistor with a multimeter
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect the thermistor wire harness. Set your multimeter to ohms and measure the resistance across the thermistor terminals. At room temperature (approximately 77 degrees F / 25 degrees C), a typical Samsung thermistor reads around 10,000 ohms (10 kohms). Readings significantly different from this suggest a faulty sensor.

fixSteps.title: Replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the old thermistor from its mounting location on the sump. This usually involves releasing a clip or removing a small screw. Install the new thermistor, secure it in position, and reconnect the wire harness.

whenToCallPro: If the new thermistor reads correctly but the tE error persists, the temperature monitoring circuit on the main control board is likely damaged and requires professional replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher tE temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: What resistance should my Samsung dishwasher thermistor read?
faq.a: At room temperature (about 77 degrees F / 25 degrees C), most Samsung dishwasher thermistors read approximately 10,000 ohms (10 kohms). As temperature increases, resistance decreases. At 150 degrees F, it typically reads around 2,000-3,000 ohms. Consult your model's service manual for exact values.

faq.q: Can a faulty thermistor cause the dishwasher to overheat?
faq.a: Potentially, yes. If the thermistor reads a falsely low temperature, the control board may continue to power the heating element beyond safe levels. However, most Samsung dishwashers have a secondary thermal fuse that will cut power to the heater as a safety backup.

faq.q: Is the thermistor the same as the heating element?
faq.a: No. The thermistor is a small temperature-sensing device that measures water temperature. The heating element is the large component that actually heats the water. They work together: the thermistor tells the control board what the temperature is, and the board decides whether to turn the heater on or off.

title: Wi-Fi / SmartThings Connection Mode
shortDescription: The dishwasher is in Access Point (AP) mode for Wi-Fi setup and SmartThings connection, not an actual error code.
fullDescription: The AP display is not an error code but an indication that the dishwasher has entered Access Point mode for Wi-Fi configuration. In this mode, the dishwasher broadcasts its own Wi-Fi network so that a smartphone with the Samsung SmartThings app can connect to it and configure the home network credentials. This mode is activated manually during initial smart-home setup or when reconfiguring the network settings. If you did not intentionally initiate this mode, a simple power cycle will exit AP mode.

causes.cause: User activated AP mode through the control panel for Wi-Fi setup
AP mode was triggered accidentally by pressing a specific button combination
Previous Wi-Fi configuration was lost after a power outage or reset
Firmware glitch causing the dishwasher to enter AP mode unexpectedly

symptoms: AP displayed on the control panel
Dishwasher will not start a wash cycle while in AP mode
A new Wi-Fi network named 'Samsung DW' or similar appears on nearby devices
SmartThings app prompts to connect to the dishwasher

fixSteps.title: Exit AP mode with a power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: If you did not intend to set up Wi-Fi, simply unplug the dishwasher for 30 seconds and plug it back in. This will exit AP mode and return the dishwasher to normal operation.

fixSteps.title: Complete the Wi-Fi setup (if desired)
fixSteps.instruction: If you want to connect the dishwasher to your home Wi-Fi, download the Samsung SmartThings app on your phone. Open the app, tap Add Device, select Samsung Dishwasher, and follow the on-screen instructions to connect to the dishwasher's AP network and enter your home Wi-Fi credentials.

fixSteps.title: Reset network settings
fixSteps.instruction: If you previously connected the dishwasher to Wi-Fi and want to reconfigure, press and hold the Smart Control or Wi-Fi button on the control panel for 3-5 seconds until AP appears. Then proceed with the SmartThings setup process.

fixSteps.title: Update dishwasher firmware
fixSteps.instruction: After connecting to Wi-Fi through the SmartThings app, check for available firmware updates. Go to the device settings in the app and look for a firmware update option. Updating firmware can resolve glitches that cause unexpected AP mode entry.

whenToCallPro: There is no need to call a professional for the AP mode display. It is a user-initiated setup mode, not an error. If the dishwasher repeatedly enters AP mode on its own, a firmware update through SmartThings should resolve it.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher AP mode Wi-Fi setup SmartThings

faq.q: Is AP an error code?
faq.a: No. AP stands for Access Point mode. It means the dishwasher is broadcasting a Wi-Fi signal for smart-home setup. It is not an error and does not indicate any malfunction.

faq.q: How do I exit AP mode?
faq.a: Simply unplug the dishwasher for 30 seconds and plug it back in. The dishwasher will return to its normal operating mode.

faq.q: Do all Samsung dishwashers support Wi-Fi?
faq.a: No. Wi-Fi and SmartThings connectivity is available on select Samsung dishwasher models, typically mid-range and premium models manufactured from 2016 onward. Check your model specifications to confirm Wi-Fi support.

title: Over-Level Water Warning (Alternate Display)
shortDescription: An alternate display of the over-level water error, indicating excessive water in the dishwasher tub.
fullDescription: The Oc display is an alternate rendering of the OC (over-level) error code that may appear on certain Samsung dishwasher models or display configurations. Some models display the code with a lowercase 'c', but the meaning is identical to the uppercase OC error. The dishwasher has detected water levels above the safe operating threshold. This can be caused by a stuck-open inlet valve, a drain blockage, or a faulty water level sensor. Immediate action is required to prevent potential water damage.

causes.cause: Water inlet valve stuck in the open position
Drain system obstruction preventing proper water evacuation
Water level sensor malfunction reporting incorrect levels
Control board error in managing the fill and drain cycles

symptoms: Oc or OC displayed on the control panel
Excess water visible in the dishwasher tub
Water leaking from the door area
Dishwasher will not start new cycles

fixSteps.title: Turn off the water supply immediately
fixSteps.instruction: Close the hot water supply valve under the kitchen sink to stop additional water from entering the dishwasher. Unplug the dishwasher or turn off its circuit breaker.

fixSteps.title: Drain excess water from the tub
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher door carefully. Use a cup, towels, or a wet/dry vacuum to remove excess water from the tub until the water level is below the door seal.

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter and check the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the drain filter. Inspect the drain hose for kinks or clogs. Ensure the garbage disposal inlet is clear. Restore power and run a drain-only cycle if available.

fixSteps.title: Test the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher. Turn the water supply on. If water continues to enter the tub while the dishwasher is unpowered, the inlet valve is stuck open and must be replaced.

whenToCallPro: If the inlet valve and drain system check out fine, a technician should inspect the water level sensor and control board for faults.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher Oc overflow error code fix

faq.q: Is Oc different from OC?
faq.a: No. The Oc display is simply an alternate rendering of the OC error code on certain Samsung models. The meaning, causes, and troubleshooting are identical.

faq.q: Should I be concerned about water damage with the Oc error?
faq.a: Yes. The Oc error means water levels are dangerously high. Take immediate action by shutting off the water supply to prevent overflow and potential damage to your kitchen floor and cabinetry.

faq.q: Can I run the dishwasher normally after clearing the Oc error?
faq.a: Only if the underlying cause has been resolved. If you simply clear the error without fixing the root problem (stuck valve, clogged drain, etc.), the error will recur and you risk flooding.

title: Heavy Cycle Indicator Flashing - Water Heating Issue
shortDescription: The Heavy cycle light flashing indicates the dishwasher is having trouble heating water to the required temperature.
fullDescription: When the Heavy cycle indicator LED flashes during operation, it signals that the dishwasher's water temperature has not reached the expected level within the allotted time. Unlike a specific alphanumeric error code, this flashing indicator is used on some Samsung dishwasher models that have a simplified display without a full alphanumeric panel. The Heavy light flashing is functionally equivalent to a heater or temperature sensor error (HE/tE). The dishwasher may continue to run but with reduced cleaning effectiveness since the water is not reaching optimal temperature.

causes.cause: Incoming water supply temperature is too low
Heating element has burned out or is degraded
Temperature sensor (thermistor) malfunction
Wiring fault between heater and control board

symptoms: Heavy cycle LED flashing on the control panel
Water inside the dishwasher feels cold or lukewarm after a wash cycle
Dishes are not properly cleaned with greasy residue
Cycle runs longer than normal

fixSteps.title: Run hot water before starting the dishwasher
fixSteps.instruction: Before starting the dishwasher, run the kitchen faucet on hot until the water feels hot (at least 120 degrees F). This ensures hot water is available in the supply line when the dishwasher begins filling.

fixSteps.title: Check your water heater temperature setting
fixSteps.instruction: Verify your home water heater is set to at least 120 degrees F. If it is set lower, increase the temperature and wait for the tank to reheat before running the dishwasher.

fixSteps.title: Test the heating element
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher. Access the heating element terminals from below. Disconnect the wires and test continuity with a multimeter. A good element reads 10-30 ohms. An open reading means the element needs replacement.

fixSteps.title: Test and replace the thermistor
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the thermistor on the sump assembly. Disconnect its connector and test resistance with a multimeter. At room temperature, expect approximately 10 kohms. If the reading is significantly off, replace the thermistor.

whenToCallPro: If the heating element and thermistor both test good, and hot water is being supplied to the dishwasher, the control board's heater circuit may be faulty. A professional should diagnose the board.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher Heavy light flashing heater error

faq.q: Is the Heavy light flashing an error code?
faq.a: Not technically. It is a diagnostic indicator used on Samsung dishwashers with simplified displays that do not have alphanumeric error code screens. The flashing Heavy light indicates a water heating issue equivalent to the HE or tE error codes.

faq.q: Can cold weather cause the Heavy light to flash?
faq.a: Indirectly, yes. In cold weather, the incoming water temperature may be significantly lower than normal. If the hot water heater cannot keep up or the supply pipes are very cold, the dishwasher's heater may not raise the water temperature fast enough, triggering the indicator.

faq.q: Should I stop using the dishwasher if the Heavy light is flashing?
faq.a: You can continue to use the dishwasher, but cleaning performance will be reduced due to the lower water temperature. It is best to resolve the issue for optimal cleaning and sanitization.

title: Normal and Delicate Lights Flashing - Water Supply Issue
shortDescription: The Normal and Delicate cycle indicators flashing simultaneously indicate a water supply problem.
fullDescription: When the Normal and Delicate cycle LED indicators flash simultaneously on Samsung dishwashers with simplified display panels, it indicates a water supply error. This is functionally equivalent to the 4C or 4E error code on models with alphanumeric displays. The dishwasher is unable to fill with water within the expected timeframe. The combined flashing pattern is Samsung's method of communicating specific errors on models that lack a full digital display. The most common causes are a closed water supply valve, kinked supply hose, or clogged inlet valve screen.

causes.cause: Water supply valve not fully open
Kinked or obstructed water supply hose
Inlet valve screen clogged with sediment
Failed water inlet valve solenoid

symptoms: Normal and Delicate cycle LEDs flashing at the same time
No sound of water filling the dishwasher when a cycle starts
Dishwasher does not begin washing after cycle selection
Other cycle LEDs remain solid or off

fixSteps.title: Open the water supply valve
