fixSteps.instruction: Check under the kitchen sink and ensure the water supply valve that feeds the dishwasher is fully open (turned counterclockwise). Try restarting the dishwasher cycle.

fixSteps.title: Inspect the supply hose for kinks
fixSteps.instruction: Pull the dishwasher out carefully to inspect the rear supply hose. Look for kinks, bends, or crushed sections. Straighten the hose so water can flow freely.

fixSteps.title: Clean the inlet valve filter
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the water supply. Disconnect the supply hose from the dishwasher inlet valve. Remove the small mesh screen inside the valve connection, clean it of debris, and reinstall.

fixSteps.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixSteps.instruction: If the supply is clear but the dishwasher still does not fill, test the inlet valve solenoid with a multimeter (50-500 ohms expected). If out of range, replace the inlet valve.

whenToCallPro: If the water supply is confirmed and the inlet valve has been replaced but the lights continue to flash, a technician should inspect the control board and water level sensor circuit.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher Normal Delicate lights flashing water supply fix

faq.q: Why do the Normal and Delicate lights flash instead of showing an error code?
faq.a: Some Samsung dishwasher models have simplified LED control panels without alphanumeric displays. These models communicate errors through specific LED flashing patterns. The Normal+Delicate combination corresponds to the 4C/4E water supply error.

faq.q: How do I identify other flashing light errors on my Samsung dishwasher?
faq.a: Samsung provides a flashing light guide in the user manual for models with LED-only displays. Different combinations of flashing lights correspond to different error conditions. Refer to your specific model's manual for the complete list.

faq.q: Can I still use the dishwasher if these lights are flashing?
faq.a: No. The flashing lights indicate the dishwasher cannot fill with water and will not run a cycle. You need to resolve the water supply issue before the dishwasher will operate.

title: Heavy and Quick Lights Flashing - Drain Error
shortDescription: The Heavy and Quick cycle indicators flashing together signal a drain problem, equivalent to the 5C/5E error code.
fullDescription: When the Heavy and Quick cycle LED indicators flash simultaneously on Samsung dishwashers with simplified display panels, it indicates a drain error. This is functionally equivalent to the 5C or 5E error code on models with alphanumeric displays. The dishwasher is unable to drain water from the tub within the expected timeframe. This flashing pattern is Samsung's method of communicating specific errors on models that do not have a full digital display. The most common causes are a clogged drain filter, a blocked drain hose, or a failed drain pump.

causes.cause: Clogged drain filter with food debris and grease
Blocked or kinked drain hose
Garbage disposal drain connection blocked
Drain pump motor failure

symptoms: Heavy and Quick cycle LEDs flashing simultaneously
Standing water remains in the bottom of the tub
Dishwasher stops mid-cycle and does not advance
Unpleasant odor from stagnant water in the tub

fixSteps.title: Clean the drain filter
fixSteps.instruction: Open the dishwasher and remove the lower rack. Twist the cylindrical drain filter counterclockwise to remove it. Rinse under running water, using a soft brush to clear grease and food particles. Clean the filter housing inside the tub.

fixSteps.title: Check and clear the drain hose
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the drain hose running from the dishwasher to the sink drain or garbage disposal. Look for kinks or clogs. Disconnect the hose and flush it with water to clear any obstructions.

fixSteps.title: Clear the garbage disposal
fixSteps.instruction: If connected to a garbage disposal, run the disposal to clear accumulated debris. Verify the dishwasher drain port on the disposal is not blocked. On new installations, ensure the knockout plug has been removed.

fixSteps.title: Test the drain pump
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug the dishwasher and access the drain pump from underneath. Disconnect the electrical connector and test for continuity (15-30 ohms typical). If the pump shows no continuity, replace it.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning the filter and drain path does not resolve the issue, and the drain pump tests bad, a professional can replace the pump and inspect for deeper blockages in the sump area.
videoSearchQuery: Samsung dishwasher Heavy Quick lights flashing drain error fix

faq.q: What does it mean when Heavy and Quick flash at the same time?
faq.a: This flashing pattern is Samsung's way of indicating a drain error on dishwasher models with LED-only displays. It is equivalent to the 5C or 5E error code on models with alphanumeric screens.

faq.q: How do I manually drain the dishwasher with standing water?
faq.a: You can scoop water out with a cup, soak it up with towels, or use a wet/dry vacuum. You can also try lowering the drain hose into a bucket below dishwasher level to gravity-drain the water.

faq.q: Should I clean the filter regularly to prevent this issue?
faq.a: Yes. Samsung recommends cleaning the drain filter at least once a month. If you run the dishwasher daily or frequently wash heavily soiled dishes, clean the filter more often to prevent buildup that leads to drain problems.
